title: amsinckia tessellata var. gloriosa
intro: Amsinckia tessellata var. gloriosa, also known as Western Desert Fiddleneck, is a flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. It is a member of the borage family and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and expectorant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its root has been used to treat digestive problems and fever. Amsinckia tessellata var. gloriosa can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine.
